trivia_bullets.text: Duskmourn: House of Horror (DSK, 2024-09-27) is set on a plane that is a single supernatural haunted house. The set draws on 1970s-1990s horror cinema including The Texas Chain Saw Massacre, The Shining, and The Blair Witch Project.
sources: Wizards.com: Duskmourn House of Horror Mechanics (Matt Tabak, 2024-08-31)
GamesRadar: How MTG Duskmourn captures the 80s horror vibe

trivia_bullets.text: "Come. Strip yourself of flesh and fear. Hunt for me. Entertain me." — The Lord of Pain. The Lord of Pain is the de facto leader of the razorkin, existing only within the House's screens, coaxing survivors into conversion. His design draws on John Kramer (Jigsaw, Saw) and Pinhead (Hellraiser).
sources: MTG Wiki: Lord of Pain, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Give In to Violence is a {1}{B} instant granting +2/+2 and lifelink until end of turn. Legal in Pauper, Standard, Pioneer, Modern, Commander. EDHREC rank 13,722 — functional combat trick, middle-of-the-road inclusion for the DSK black commons.
Illustrated by Septian Fajrianto, an Indonesian digital artist active across recent MTG sets. No published artist spotlight found as of this research pass.

related_cards.name: The Lord of Pain
related_cards.note: Named speaker of the flavor text; his lore grounds the card's seduction-into-violence framing

related_cards.name: Razorkin Needlehead
related_cards.note: Illustrates the razorkin faction the Lord of Pain commands

related_cards.name: Chainsaw
related_cards.note: Same horror-cinema design frame — direct 1980s slasher reference in the same set
